title: Linear Models, Regression, and Systems · Model Lens: Linear Rate Intercept
summary: Allocate staff and supplies under multiple linear constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int linear rate intercept increment in a linear allocation and scenario model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.
Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Linear Rate Intercept evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (1, 5) and (5, 17). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Linear Rate Intercept for a linear allocation and scenario model.
steps.why: The Linear Rate Intercept result for a linear allocation and scenario model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.
